findings.evidence: `rg` shows `blockLaw` and `blockLaw_apply` are defined in `code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Basic.lean:37` and `:84`; the new file only imports `TauCeti.Probability.Exchangeability.Basic` and `Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Integral.Lebesgue.Basic` at `Rectangles.lean:3-4`, and none of the three theorem statements mention any DeFinetti/directing-measure API.
findings.fix: Move them to an Exchangeability/block-law rectangle file, e.g. `TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Rectangles.lean` importing `Exchangeability.Basic` and the lintegral module, or place the non-integral block-law lemma near `blockLaw_apply` in `Exchangeability/Basic.lean` if that import cost is acceptable.
findings.issue: `blockLaw_apply_rectangle`, `indicator_product_eq_indicator_rectangle_preimage`, and `lintegral_indicator_product_eq_blockLaw_rectangle` are generic finite-block/indicator facts, not DeFinetti-specific material.

summary: The new lemmas are elementary block-law/rectangle API, but the file places them under DeFinetti even though they only depend on Layer 0 `Exchangeability.Basic` plus lintegral facts. Move this API to an exchangeability/block-law home so users do not import DeFinetti for generic finite-dimensional law facts.
